reason: Multiple dedicated MCP servers exist for NotebookLM that expose its capabilities (notebook management, source ingestion, grounded queries, and audio overview generation) over the Model Context Protocol. These are community-built implementations that use reverse-engineered APIs with browser automation for authentication. Examples include jacob-bd/notebooklm-mcp-cli, Cezial/notebooklm-mcp-server, myakwang/notebooklm-mcp, mulyg/notebooklm-mcp, and PleasePrompto/notebooklm-mcp.
